summary.intent: Improve the LLM provider/model listing UI to be less overwhelming by collapsing long model lists, sorting by recency/popularity, and surfacing probe errors more clearly.
summary.outcome: Implemented collapsible model lists (top 3 visible, expand toggle), version-aware sorting for models without dates, a `recommended` field on `ModelInfo`, legacy model hiding (>1yr) from chat selector, and inline probe error display with the actual server error message instead of a generic localized one.

repo: The project uses three separate JS files for model card rendering: `page-providers.js` (settings), `onboarding-view.js` (onboarding), and `providers.js` (preferred models modal) — changes must be applied to all three independently.
All WS/RPC error responses are localized in `ws-connect.js` via `localizeRpcError` before reaching resolve callbacks, which was overwriting specific server error messages with generic locale strings.

The project has two biome versions: a system `biome` 2.4.6 (via mise) used by `local-validate.sh`, and `npx biome` 0.3.3 — formatting rules differ, `biome check --write` with the wrong version doesn't fix CI failures.
Static model catalogs use `catalog_to_discovered(CATALOG, N)` where N controls how many entries get `recommended: true` — first N entries in the array are flagged as recommended.

The `ServiceError::Message` variant always serializes with `code: UNAVAILABLE` regardless of the actual error content (see service-traits/src/lib.rs), which is why permission errors looked like service unavailability.
Config validation schema entries must be kept in sync in `validate.rs` when adding new fields to config structs — there's a schema map that must be manually updated.

code.finding: Every WS frame's error is localized here via `localizeRpcError` before reaching the pending promise resolve, overwriting specific server error messages with generic locale strings.
All non-Forbidden `ServiceError` variants serialize with `code: UNAVAILABLE`, meaning specific error messages get masked by locale lookups downstream.
localizedRpcErrorMessage previously ignored error.message entirely when a code translation existed — fixed to preserve serverMessage before localization.
OpenAI API-discovered models get `recommended: true` via pattern matching on known flagship IDs (gpt-5.4, o4-mini, o3 without date suffixes) in parse_model_entry.

models.list() filters out models older than 1 year unless preferred or lacking a timestamp. models.list_all() (used by settings page) skips this filter. The preferred field was missing from list_all responses and had to be added.

workflow: Always run `biome` from the mise-managed system binary (2.4.6), not `npx biome` — the versions have different formatting rules that cause CI failures.

When a Rust struct gains a new non-optional field, test code constructions will break with missing-field errors — a bulk sed on `created_at:` patterns works to add `recommended: false` to test blocks, but must not touch struct definitions.

The probe error flow: backend log error → ServiceError::Message → UNAVAILABLE code → ws-connect localizeRpcError → overwrites message. Preserving the original as `serverMessage` on the error object is the cleanest fix without breaking other error display.

friction: Three separate model card rendering code paths (settings, onboarding, preferred modal) meant every UI fix had to be applied three times — discovered incrementally as the user tested each view.
The bulk sed to add `recommended: false` to ModelInfo struct initializations accidentally modified a `PendingInvoke` struct in server.rs that also had a `created_at` field pattern.
Biome version mismatch between `npx biome` (0.3.3) and system mise biome (2.4.6) caused CI lint failures that local `biome check --write` didn't catch or fix.

The WS error localization layer was completely invisible — it took multiple rounds of HTML/log comparison to discover that `ws-connect.js` was silently overwriting the actual error message before it reached any business logic.
ModelInfo had 9 construction sites in providers/src/lib.rs alone, requiring many targeted edits; some were in destructuring patterns that also needed updating.
open_items: The `recommended` field for static catalogs beyond Anthropic, OpenAI Codex, and a few others may need tuning — GitHub Copilot and OpenAI-compatible providers use a generic top-N approach rather than curated flagship selection.
The `providers.show_legacy_models` config option was added but not yet surfaced in the settings UI — users can only enable it via moltis.toml.
Popularity-based sorting (pre-selection, before user has any usage data) was scoped to static `recommended` flags; a community/crowdsourced popularity signal was discussed but not implemented.
The `humanizeProbeError` function in provider-validation.js still has some heuristic patterns that may be too generic or mismatch edge cases across providers.
The Moonshot/MiniMax/Z.AI providers use a generic `catalog_to_discovered(CATALOG, 2)` for recommended models — these may benefit from provider-specific flagship curation.
